title: NOC 72100 – Machinists and machining and tooling inspectors | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, Machinists and Tooling Inspectors (NOC 72100) are the precision-engineering experts at the heart of Canada’s "Advanced Manufacturing" push. As the country expands its aerospace, EV battery, and medical device sectors, these professionals are responsible for crafting high-tolerance components using cutting-edge 5-axis CNC machines and robotic machining cells. Classified as TEER 2, this role has evolved from traditional manual lathing to a high-tech discipline requiring advanced digital literacy, 3D spatial reasoning, and the ability to work with exotic new composite materials and "Smart Alloys."

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Category2 (Requires college, apprenticeship, or significant experience)Major Group72 – Technical trades and transportation officersSub-Major Group721 – Machining, metal forming, shaping and erecting trades

duties: Plan & Sequence: Reading and interpreting complex engineering drawings and blueprints to determine the best sequence of machining operations.
Precision Operation: Setting up and operating a variety of machine tools (CNC and manual) to perform precision sawing, turning, milling, and grinding.
Verification: Using precision measuring instruments like micrometers, verniers, and Coordinate Measuring Machines (CMM) to verify tolerances to the micron level.
Quality Control (Inspectors): Reporting deviations from specifications, maintaining calibration of measuring tools, and completing detailed inspection reports.
2026 Update: Programming machine tools using advanced CAM (Computer-Aided Manufacturing) software and monitoring real-time sensor data for tool wear.
employment_requirements: Education: Completion of secondary school is the standard requirement.
Apprenticeship: Completion of a four-year apprenticeship program OR a combination of over four years of work experience and some college/industry courses is usually required for trade certification.
Certification: Trade certification for machinists is available but voluntary in all provinces except Quebec. Red Seal endorsement is highly recommended for mobility and higher pay.
Inspector Requirements: Machining and tooling inspectors require several years of experience as a skilled machinist.
labour_market_demand: The 2026 outlook is Balanced to Strong, with a critical shortage of "Master Machinists" capable of programming complex CNC workflows:

Ontario: Very Good prospects. In February and March 2026, Ontario issued targeted OINP invitations for NOC 72100 under the Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker stream. Minimum scores for these trades draws started as low as 50, reflecting the province's urgent need for machinists in the automotive and industrial heartlands.

Alberta: Strong demand. Machinists here earn some of the highest wages in Canada, with top-tier industrial roles in Fort McMurray and Edmonton reaching $65.00–$80.00/hr to support heavy mining and energy infrastructure.

British Columbia: Good prospects. Focused in Metro Vancouver and the Kootenays for specialized marine and mining equipment repair. BC projects over 1,500 openings through 2035 due to a massive retirement wave.

National Trend: Over 97% of workers in this NOC receive comprehensive non-wage benefits. The 2026 market highly favors candidates with Red Seal certification or specialized experience in CNC Programming.
Why Work in Canada as a Machinist?
Technological Innovation: Gain hands-on experience with 2026-grade technologies, including Augmented Reality (AR) maintenance overlays and AI-driven predictive tooling sensors.
Excellent Stability: 81% of machinists work all year round—much higher than the national average—providing long-term financial security.
High-Tier Compensation: As a TEER 2 trade, your earning potential is high, with a national median wage of $34.00/hr and specialized seniors earning over $120,000 annually.
Priority for PR: NOC 72100 is a core occupation in the 2026 Express Entry "Trade Occupations" category and is frequently targeted by Ontario's provincial nomination draws.
pathways: OINP Employer Job Offer: In February 2026, OINP issued invitations to candidates in NOC 72100 with a score of 50+ under the Foreign Worker stream. This is a prime route for those with an Ontario job offer.

2026 Express Entry (Trade Occupations Category): IRCC has renewed the "Trade Occupations" category for 2026. Note that the minimum work experience requirement has been increased to 12 months (up from 6 months in 2025).
2026 TR to PR Pathway: A newly confirmed initiative targeting up to 33,000 temporary residents. Machinists are classified as "High-Priority Essential Manufacturing Workers."
BC PNP Skilled Worker: A reliable route for machinists with a high-wage offer in BC's industrial hubs, particularly those with Red Seal certification.
